name: api-testing
description:
Use when writing tests for backend APIs or frontend flows.
Triggers for: unit tests, integration tests, E2E tests, pytest fixtures,
TestClient setup, mock data factories, or test coverage analysis.
NOT for: testing business logic that doesn't involve API endpoints.
capabilities: Create pytest fixtures for FastAPI TestClient and database sessions
Write unit tests for pure business logic and service functions
Write integration tests for API endpoints with authentication
Configure Playwright E2E tests for React/Next.js frontends
Set up CI/CD test pipelines with coverage reporting
Create test data fixtures in JSON format
author: Awais68
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The scanner incorrectly flagged regex functions (re.match/re.search) as weak cryptographic algorithms. Environment variable references and hardcoded URLs are documentation examples for test configuration, not credential exposure. The skill follows standard API testing patterns with no malicious behavior.
valueStatement: Developers struggle to create reliable tests for their APIs. This skill provides ready-to-use patterns for unit, integration, and E2E tests using pytest and Playwright.
userTitle: Write API Tests for FastAPI and React
limitations: Does not generate tests for non-API code like pure frontend components
Does not create load testing or performance benchmarks
Does not set up test databases in production environments
Does not generate tests for GraphQL APIs

useCases.title: Add tests to a new FastAPI endpoint
useCases.description: Create pytest fixtures and integration tests for a new student CRUD endpoint with authentication and validation.

useCases.title: Set up E2E tests for a React dashboard
useCases.description: Configure Playwright with Page Object Model for testing user flows like login, student creation, and filtering.

useCases.title: Add tests to CI/CD pipeline
useCases.description: Create GitHub Actions workflow that runs backend pytest with coverage and frontend Playwright tests on each push.

promptTemplates.title: Basic API test request
promptTemplates.prompt: Write pytest tests for a FastAPI endpoint at GET /api/v1/users/{user_id} that returns user details. Include tests for success case and 404 not found.
promptTemplates.scenario: Need simple unit tests for a single endpoint

promptTemplates.title: Integration test with authentication
promptTemplates.prompt: Create integration tests for the POST /api/v1/students endpoint. Use the TestClient fixture with Bearer token authentication. Test create success, unauthorized access, and validation errors.
promptTemplates.scenario: Test authenticated endpoint with multiple scenarios

promptTemplates.title: Complete test suite for module
promptTemplates.description: Generate a complete test module for the fees API including conftest.py fixtures, unit tests for calculate_fee function, and integration tests for all fee endpoints.
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a complete test module for the fees API including conftest.py fixtures, unit tests for calculate_fee function, and integration tests for all fee endpoints.
promptTemplates.scenario: Build full test coverage for a new module

promptTemplates.title: E2E test with Page Object Model
promptTemplates.prompt: Write Playwright E2E tests using Page Object Model for the student management feature. Include tests for login, create student, filter by grade, and view student details.
promptTemplates.scenario: Create maintainable E2E tests for user workflows

bestPractices: Use pytest fixtures for shared setup to avoid code duplication
Test both happy path and edge cases including validation errors
Isolate tests with fresh database for each test function
Use Page Object Model in Playwright for maintainable selectors
antiPatterns: Hardcoding test data in individual test functions instead of fixtures
Making tests depend on execution order or other tests
Using production database credentials in test configuration
Skipping cleanup of test data between test runs

faq.question: What testing frameworks does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill supports pytest for Python/FastAPI backends and Playwright for TypeScript/React frontends. It also covers Cypress as an alternative E2E framework.

faq.question: How do I test authenticated endpoints?
faq.answer: Create an auth_headers fixture that generates a JWT token using create_access_token. Pass these headers to TestClient requests for authenticated endpoints.

faq.question: Can this skill generate tests for GraphQL APIs?
faq.answer: No, this skill focuses on REST APIs with FastAPI. For GraphQL, you would need a different skill or adapt the pytest patterns to use graphene-test-client.

faq.question: How do I run tests in CI?
faq.answer: Use the provided GitHub Actions workflow example. It sets up Python/Node, installs dependencies, runs pytest with coverage, and optionally runs Playwright tests.

faq.question: Should I use an in-memory database for tests?
faq.answer: Yes, use SQLite with StaticPool for isolation and speed. The conftest.py example shows how to create an in-memory database that resets for each test.

faq.question: What coverage targets should I aim for?
faq.answer: Aim for 80%+ coverage on core modules and 90%+ on critical paths like authentication, payment processing, and data validation.
